Do not implement the "rank-per-role" system. The level at which a player plays one role is generally reflective of the level at which they play other roles; there are general gameplay mechanics, features, and necessary knowledge required across all roles. Mainly scared for the rank per role changes. While the idea sounds nice it also feels like there are some ways to cheese it. What if you are a Diamond III Toplane main assigned to Support (which you play at, say, a Silver II level) and the person who is your toplaner gets their flex champ banned so you decide to switch roles? Now your Diamond III skill can still show off in toplane despite that fact that you were placed against teams playing around the Silver II level.

The idea sounds nice in theory, but the downsides are also potentially very scary. :) I would love to see this pulled off if it can be done reasonably, but having one overall role to work towards also feels really nice. Perhaps the rank per role thing could behave more like hidden MMR, such that if you as a Gold 4 (Overall rank with only one shown rank) ADC Main are filled to midlane that you play around a Silver 5 level you'll be placed around a Silver 5 midlaner as well? There's some tradeoffs to that idea as well but I figured I'd just bring it to the table.

Irelia in the distance: 💃🕺💃💃🕺🕺

Watching her closely, I notice that the blade movement changes with the slight move of her hand.
When her hand is angled downwards, the blades slow down, and when her hand is nearly vertical, the blade movement speeds up immensely. When her pointer and middle finger are close together, the blades stay close together. When her fingers are separated, the blades move farther apart.
Great touch.

The crescendo- could be either a battle or her dancing throughout Ionia with the blades following.
The quiet bit after- maybe getting knocked by sion as it slowly builds as she remembers what she is fighting for and will not give up.
The final crescendo- Fighting alongside fellow Ionians and refusing to let anyone take their land.
A year later and I still get the same goosebumps as the first time

HOW COULD RIOT BE CAPITALIZING ON LORE MORE THAN THEY ARE?

     Riot spends so much time with these environments and cultures they develop as really unnecessary extras, but in reality it does add a layer of depth to each character. Honestly the more effort riot puts into lore, the more I personally want to play certain champions. While detail may be beneficial for backstory purposes, am I really the only one that thinks that its being underutilized?
     Hear me out. Imagine if League of Legends could capitalize on the different environments/cultures (i.e. Bildgewater, Ionia, Demacia, Noxus, etc.) the same way Call of Duty did. Granted the majority of CoD was based on historical events; but still, even the recent Call of Duty installments have shown that it's at least possible to have completely hypothetical multiplayer maps based around a center plot line. And CoD isn't the only franchise to do this. Look at the success games like Halo and Battlefront have had.
     Imagine if the entire aspect of League was the same. I want to repeat that. THE ASPECT OF LEAGUE IS THE SAME. What I mean by this is that the 5v5 "Summoner's Rift" style aspect, with 6 jungle camps (including buffs), 2 epic monsters, and 2 skuttle crabs; with the same objective based gameplay of destroying towers, taking inhibitors, and eventually leading to the destruction of one team's nexus.
    The twist comes in when you think of Summoners Rift. Summoners Rift gets repetitive. Am I the only one tired of banning Camile, Tristana and Yasuo every game? Of people abusing "O.P." to compensate for lack of skill in order to climb in ranked? Picture if for, God forbid, before each match 30 seconds was dedicated to voting between 2 maps or electing to play on a map that will be picked at random. These maps would be based around the different areas of Runeterrra (i.e. Bildgewater, Ionia, Demacia, Noxus, etc.). Think about if the map interacted with champions the same way it does in other games. Like on some maps snipers are more beneficial, where as on others playing with anything other than a submachine gun or a shotgun is almost asking for suicide, but still entirely possible with the right amount of skill.
     Riot could really take this and run with it in a bunch of ways. Buffs or special itemizations  to champions that are from that area... jungle camps give different effects... but most obviously visual aesthetics. Allowing their players to play IN Bildgewater, In Ionia, In Demacia. You get the idea. With things such as background scenery, jungle and epic monster camps, turrets and even minions all tailored to look like each of these unique areas and give that epic sensation this lore is meant to inspire. This would be a great opportunity to embrace all of this lore Riot seems to be running away with right now; and very well might I add. I really appreciate the effort that goes into the lore of new champions and the revitalizing they're giving to older champions.
    Can I get some feedback or thoughts on what the community thinks about this idea? Obviously this is just an off-the-dome brainstorm and there is a lot more construction that could be done around it. I'm looking for feedback on the idea of playing on different maps other than Summoner's Rift with the same objective that Summoner's Rift offers, only the map is now based on the different areas of Runeterra and will offer advantages in some way, either for specific champions or by utilizing the various creature lore of different areas to replace normal jungle monsters.
     THOUGHTS??

I understand that there's a limit to what Riot can put into the game, but I wish they made MF's skin similar to Elementalist Lux where it's a minigame of sorts. Since Miss Fortune is a bounty hunter, adding a mechanic where you gain upgrades with each kill/milestone you get (since getting kills can take too much time, perhaps adding a system where when you reach a certain amount of gold you can "purchase" upgrades, without technically spending gold, then resetting until you reach the next milestone to buy a more expensive upgrade) allowing you to fully customize your guns and suit, perhaps introducing the four versions of MF as branches more than just suits. You could chose upgrades from each version (Scarlet Fair, Royal Arms, Starswarm, and Zero Hour) and the animations would change shape, style, and color. For example, adding an upgrade for Royal Arms and Starswarm would produce an orange-yellow-red particle animation. Elementalist Lux has 9 forms I believe, while Gun Goddess MF would have a bounty of combinations possible, making a skin that Riot can charge at the full price of an ultimate skin, and a skin that could surpass the incredibility of Elementalist Lux and attract more buyers. Anyone else think this would be a cool idea?

Tibbers is a gift by Annies mom. She loved her a lot and felt safe in the family or her, her mom and her dad. When her mom died, her father found this new wife and her step- or halfsister. In the second drawing you can clearly see, how annie doesnt feel secure in the house and like an equal family member. Since she draws her burried mom, I believe she doesnt accept the new family constellation and misses her mom a lot. Thats why tibbers, something that connects her to her mom, is so important to her, since it closes a gap which her mom left inside her, when she died. He now is her symbol of hope, strength, safety and acceptance. He is her best friend. In the last picture you can clearly see, how she wants the people she loves all back together, and how the new family members dont accept her inner feelings and wish for this. That's the problem this whole story is based on ❤

Riot Games I think forcing champions to fit into more roles would be bad for the game. That would be a lot of extra work for you guys to balance them. Don't you guys have problems balancing champions like Morgana, Zyra, and Brand because people play them both support and mid? You guys have taken certain steps to balance them as mid laners but sometimes those changes make them too strong as supports. And then, if you guys aren't careful with the process, you may end up with a multitude of champions who have no role identity because they are outdone by the competition. Take Lucian for example: he was the top ADC for a long time, then he received huge nerfs that made him a bad ADC. Then he was played mid briefly because it somehow worked, but now he is bad in both roles and feels like he doesn't have a place in the game right now.

Then look at Mordekaiser: you guys tried breaking the Marksman/Support bot lane meta by bringing in a Juggernaut to the scene, but one Juggernaut can only compete with twelve or more Marksmen if the Juggernaut absolutely crushes them. Now, Mordekaiser (ignoring the fact that he isn't that great of a pick to start with) has drifted to top lane and maybe mid because that is where he feels best, but certain tools in his kit like his W feel bad or are completely wasted because they were designed specifically for duo lanes (his W's xp splitting whatever business stuff just doesn't apply to solo lanes).

And honestly, I don't how I feel about the Morgana and Zyra changes last month. That's because I didn't see anybody actually use them in the jungle. I think I didn't play that patch the first few days and by the time I was able to, Morgana was hotfixed and that was all I heard about jungle Morgana and Zyra. I don't know, some "meta" stuff just don't make it into my games LOL.

What I'm saying is, you can't make all champions one-size-fits-all. You need to let players figure that stuff out because they have the game play knowledge. You can do certain things that can *nudge* champions in certain directions, but you cannot *push* them. Otherwise, you're breaking the game. Good day!

So many people taking this literally compared to game mechanics... The whole thing is a metaphor for struggling through the ranks from bottom to top.
Yasuo and Taliyah represent cruising through the very lower divisions. Ekko represents overconfidence in one's own abilities causing you to fall and lose. MF shows a struggle to climb back and overcome your failings. Lucian is shown wading and clawing his way through hundreds of other hopeful players who stand in his path to get to Thresh, who represents Challenger - killing Thresh is Lucian's ultimate goal in his lore, just as reaching Challenger is the ultimate goal for a serious ranked player.
Holy moly. *Movie Quality Cinematics.* I'd pay to see the movie for sure.

That scene with MF swimming frantically is genius. Not showing where she going as you see her try desperately try and swim towards something. That little snippet developed so much character with no dialogue.

Please take a moment of silence as tribute to the gone but never forgotten: 
The Blue Pill (The item used for recalling),
Zeke's Herald,
Heart of Gold,
Deathfire Grasp,
Philosopher's Stone,
Spirit of the Elder Lizard, Ancient Golem and Spectral Wraith,
Madred's Bloodrazor, 
Elixir of Agility,
Kage's Lucky Pick,
Atma's Impaler,
Avarice Blade,
Blackfire Torch,
Dervish Blade,
Boot Enchantments,
Force of Nature,
The Vision Ward,
Leviathan,
Mirage Blade,
Runic Bulwark,
Sanguine Blade,
Shard of True Ice,
Twin Shadows,
Shurelya's Reverie,
Sword of the Divine,
The Brutalizer,
Will of the Ancients,
Zephyr.
Now I know that there are many more, yet these were the ones I remembered and yes quite a few have been changed into other, more balanced, items. But it is crazy to think how much the 'Best MOBA in the world.' Has changed over the years and I hope everyone can appreciate how much work RIOT and the LoL community have put into this game.

I think the Rune System is fantastic and makes exploring a character fun, but by god there needs to be a new tree catered towards Bruisers. I think that the Precision keystones are fantastic and have their own benefit: Press the Attack provides an early game advantage with the three hit proc to do more damage and is perfect for all-in gambits. Fleet Footwork is a nice sustain option for balanced gameplay throughout the match. Lethal Tempo is great for either outputting a ton of damage late (but giving up that early game bump) or foregoing attack speed item and passive for a defensive item.

But Bruisers don't get that. Bruisers don't really need attack speed from Precision, but the Keystones in Domination and Sorcery just aren't useful for their AD, and while some health is nice, the keystones in Resolve are once again not very useful. I really think bruisers need a tree that is more useful towards them - playing them over a tank and not being able to snowball means that they're going to be much less useful once the teamfighting starts.

Also, I think Zoe is really fun, but she's definitely overtuned. I think something worth trying is making it so that her W is limited. i.e. if she picks up a Flash, her W will have a Flash cooldown (maybe lowered since that's enormous) unless she gets a kill within a time frame after she uses her W. I also think her E should be looked at - I think it's a cool idea but it has too many positive options. It stays on the ground ridiculously long and is active during all of its duration. Maybe giving it a priming time like Nidalee/Jinx/Caitlyn traps would make it have more counterplay. There have been so many times where I dodged the skillshot (even as it comes through a wall), only for it to fall, expand, and catch me anyway.

I remember the winter of 2015. I had been playing the game for a year and only recently started to really enjoy it. Living in a place where it rarely snows, it snowed for a week straight and put me and my friend out of school together for a week. I introduced him to league on a spare computer at my grandmothers house. He slept over for the entire week as I did too. We woke up at orange rolls and pancakes for breakfast before playing league all day long. I remember looking outside and seeing the snow, looking behind me to see my best friend playing league with me and go into the living room to see my grandmother, not to mention my uncle and cousins playing league with us in the call. We ended up cherishing those memories for years to come. It was probably the best week of my life.

I've been playing and following League for 8 years now, and I come back to this video every once in a while. Every single time I end up crying. Even if the game is not perfect, even if the community is is really toxic, this was the game that made e-sports huge. League of Legends built on the foundation of CS and StarCraft, and built up a cultural background for gaming. And maybe it will not last forever, but the games to come and the companies to come will have League as a starting point, just like League had StarCraft and CounterStrike. And we, who happen to live in the age where e-sports is truly blooming can tell our grandchildren the stories of the sport of our generation, the sport that defined us, just like football defined the 20th century. And my friends, who read this, we can be proud that we live in an age where gaming is getting acknowledged. We can be proud that we are gamers. And we can thank Riot for taking e-sports to where it is now. Thank you for your time.

I was having a bad morning until i watched this and made me realize how much joy league has brought to my life since i started playing back in 2012. I've met so many people through league of legends in and out of game, after high school when all my friends drifted apart, the one's whom i am still close with have remained so because of the time we share playing league. Yea I may rage, get tilted (and a little toxic) but what keeps me playing after all these years and all the changes to the game is my love for it. I remember watching the 2016 spring finals with my gf at the time and all my dad could do was bash it, he couldn't see what I could and can, i don't see non athletes or athletes playing a video game, I see 10 people doing something they love to do, something that people all over the world have come to love, including me. So even though league of legends is a toxic unbalanced piece of shit, its my toxic and unbalanced piece of shit. Thank you Riot for making our game.

Zoe

Class: Mage Disruptor/ Midlaner

Passive: Nearby enemy champions drop coins depending on which summoner spell they've just used.Zoe can grab those coins which will refresh her summoner's cooldowns. This allows her for example to us Flash up to 6 times in a teamfight.

Q: Fire a projectile in a target location that, upon reactivation, will change it's trajectory in the direction of your cursor. If it hits an enemy champion, it will deal X amount of damage.

W: Dashes to a target location, then has the option to return to her original position by reactivating her ability.

E: Zoe surrounds herself with 3 bubbles that will automatically damage nearby enemy champions. The 3 bubbles are refreshed when Zoe uses a summoner's spell.

Ultimate: Zoe throws a disrupting bubble with very high range that will create an AOE CC, stunning affected target after a delay. The AOE takes effect a small period of time after impact.


EDIT: someone commented something I had not noticed. The bubbles that surround her do appear only after she uses summoner spells, so maybe as he suggested it's part of her Passive aswell and not her W.

I feel like this song really captures Evenlynns essence. She is like a demonic siren. She starts by luring in her chosen target. She takes her time convincing them to come to her. The music is also very alluring and lulls the viewer into a false sense of security just like how Evenlynn lures people to drop their guard around her yet the sinister undertones of the song warns us of her innate wickedness. 

Finally once her victims submits there is a brief moment in the song where the tune shifts to an almost pleasant and light moment where the singing stops and it’s only the music. Evenlynn has seduced her victim who are now hopelessly in love with this beautiful women that promises them love and a good time. 

And than BOOM comes the singing with renewed vigour as Evenlynn reveals her true demonic form to her victim and starts to torment them in horrific and unspeakable ways savouring their despair and their fear and most importantly their pain which she relishes. 

However before her climax, her victim succumbs to her torture and expires and she is left once more without satisfaction. Likewise the song abruptly ends with only the echoes of the sirens song before it ends.

I will NEVER understand people saying "play this at 2x speed it's epic"! 

The original is always so much better. The tempo of the the piece is an integral part of the composition, relaying the mood behind it or a part of the character personality. Alter it, and it loses its magic (and also begins to sound distorted).

The slow, steady beat accompanied by the strings (nyckelharpa?) sets a mood of power, strength, and unrelentness. Ornn is an ancient craftsman and smith, not a goat on cocaine. He doesn't need a fast paced theme; what he needs is a theme of the heart of mountain itself, the immovable core of earth that yields to nothing but the destruction of the foundations of the world as a whole. He's patiently standing his ground, weathering the storm just like the mountain itself, unphased by anything, which is also signified by his hum in the background. And that's exactly what Riot created; a piece that perfectly encapsulates not only his prowess as a craftsman or personality, but also the timelessness of him and his work.

(not talking about his in-game performance in any way, just from the lore standpoint. I don't even play the game)

To be fair, you have to have a very high IQ to understand Honor IRL. The humour is extremely subtle, and without a solid grasp of theoretical physics most of the jokes will go over a typical player’s head. There’s also Jankos's nihilistic outlook, which is deftly woven into his characterisation- his personal philosophy draws heavily from Narodnaya Volya literature, for instance. The fans understand this stuff; they have the intellectual capacity to truly appreciate the depths of this cat, to realise that its not just funny- its a symbol that says something deep about Honor. As a consequence people who dislike League of legends truly ARE idiots- of course they wouldn’t appreciate, for instance, the humour in the typical player’s existential catchphrase “OMG NOOB TEAM!1,” which itself is a cryptic reference to Turgenev’s Russian epic Fathers and Sons. I’m smirking right now just imagining one of those addlepated Bronze 5 players scratching their heads in confusion as Phreak’s genius wit unfolds itself on their Computer screens. What fools.. how I pity them. 😂

For those who wondering why Janna has a sword, it is a common idea in almost all Chinese "Wuxia" fictions (where the idea of the skin came from) that highest level of skills is "Swordman and Swords become one" (人剑合一). Thus, you don't need sword to perform magic attacks. Instead, you use "Qi" (气).



Update: One of the designers of this skin, Charles Liu admitted that the sword is not Janna's. She is the keeper or defender of this supreme sword, thus she is not going to use it.
